common.name: Name
home.userNotFound: User record not found in database.
home.noRole: No Auditor, CS, or Senior CS role found for this account.
members.title: Members
members.description: Mark people as inactive to hide them across the entire system. They can be reactivated at any time.
members.conflictDesc: An active member named {0} already exists. Change the name, or add an External ID to distinguish them.
members.nameStillTaken: That name is still taken. Change the name or add an External ID.
members.deactivateTitle: Mark as inactive — hides from all pages
payments.title: Payments
payments.subtitle: Track all payment methods and money-in-bank status
staffperm.title: Staff PC Permissions
staffperm.subtitle: Control which PCs each auditor is allowed to work with
staffperm.allApproved: All staff PC additions have been approved.
sessions.title: Session & CS Review Approval
sessions.subtitle: Sessions and CS reviews are managed separately

courses.subtitle: Manage available courses for students
stats.noSessionData: No session data for this week.
stats.noSessionDataWeek: No session data for this week
settings.passwordTooShort: New password must be at least 8 characters.
settings.imageFormats: JPEG · PNG · GIF · WebP · max 2 MB
error.oops: Oops, the page you are trying to access does not exist ?
error.description: The requested page is not available. It might have been relocated, deleted, or never existed.
auditors.title: Auditors
auditors.noSelection: Select an auditor from the list
pcs.noSelection: Select a PC from the list
guide.hero.title: LPM System Guide
guide.hero.sub: A comprehensive guide to LifePower Management — covering auditing sessions, PC folders, academy, statistics, and administration. Follow the sections below to learn every feature.
guide.overview.title: Overview
guide.overview.lead: LPM is organised into main pages, management tools, and administration panels — all accessible from the left sidebar. Your role determines which pages are visible.
guide.overview.dashboardDesc: Your weekly session grid. Track auditing hours, CS reviews, and solo sessions for each staff member across the Thu-Wed week.
guide.overview.addSessionDesc: A 3-step wizard to create sessions: choose a PC, upload files and fill the auditor report, then write the next C/S sheet.
guide.overview.pcFolderDesc: Browse and manage all documents in a PC’s folder — Front Cover, Back Cover, and WorkSheets — with a built-in PDF viewer.
guide.overview.pcsDesc: Manage client profiles, purchases, payment history, session records, and course enrolments.
guide.overview.academyDesc: Record daily student visits, enrol students in courses, and print weekly attendance reports.
guide.overview.statisticsDesc: Visual charts, staff leaderboard, and data views summarising key metrics across the system.
guide.overview.managementDesc: Manage auditor profiles, course definitions, and the full member directory.
guide.overview.adminDesc: Work review and approval, client purchases, incoming payments, staff permissions, and messaging. Admin role required.
guide.overview.tip: Your role (Admin or regular staff) determines which pages you can see. If a page is missing from your menu, contact your administrator.
guide.gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
guide.gettingStarted.sub: Log in, install the app, and navigate the interface
guide.gettingStarted.step1text: On the login page, type your username and password, then click Sign In. If the credentials are incorrect, an error message appears below the form.
guide.gettingStarted.step2text: On Android, open LP in Chrome, tap the three-dot menu, and select "Install app". This adds LP to your home screen and enables sharing scanned PDFs directly to LP from other apps.
guide.gettingStarted.step3text: Click the eye icon inside the password field to toggle visibility. This is useful for verifying what you typed before signing in.
guide.dashboard.title: Dashboard
guide.dashboard.sub: Weekly session tracking for auditors and CS
guide.dashboard.step1text: Use the Prev / Next buttons at the top to move between weeks. The week always runs Thursday to Wednesday. The current week is shown by default.
guide.dashboard.step2text: Each column is a PC (client). Each row is a day. Cells show the total billed session time (h:mm). The dashboard displays three colour-coded tables: Auditor (blue), CS Solo (purple), and CS (grey), each with its own subtotal. A Grand Total bar at the bottom sums Auditor + CS Solo time.
guide.dashboard.step3text: Below the main grid, the MY CS Queue table lists PCs assigned to you for case supervision this week, showing their current status and last session date.
guide.dashboard.step4text: The My Auditing Status table shows all PCs you have audited this week, with sortable columns for PC Name, Date, and Duration.
guide.dashboard.step5text: If you are both a regular and solo auditor, a Solo/Both toggle appears at the top. Switch modes to view or record solo sessions separately from regular auditing time.
guide.dashboard.step6text: Click the Messages icon in the header to view your inbox. You can read messages from admins, send new messages to staff, and click "OK All" to acknowledge all pending messages at once.
guide.dashboard.step7text: Click Assign PC, then choose a category: Auditor, CS Solo, or CS. PCs with pending reviews are highlighted in green. Select the PC name from the dropdown to add it to your weekly list.
guide.dashboard.step8text: Below the tables, a text area lets you write free-form remarks for the current week. Remarks are saved automatically when you click away. A bar chart at the bottom shows your total weekly hours over the last 20 weeks.
guide.addSession.title: Add Session Wizard
guide.addSession.step1text: Select a PC from the dropdown list. If this is a free (unpaid) session, check the Free Session checkbox. Then click Next to proceed.
guide.addSession.step2text: Upload your session PDF and any attachments. You can drag and drop files onto the upload area, or click to browse. Use the "Pick from Inbox" button to select files that were shared to LP from CamScanner or other scanner apps.
guide.addSession.step3text: Fill in the handling blocks: Beginning of Handling, TITS, processes, EOS, and End of Handling. The session length is auto-calculated from the blocks, excluding gaps between them. Click the + Block button to add more blocks. Use arrow keys to navigate between cells quickly.
guide.addSession.step4text: Use the F/N, RT, YT, and PT buttons to mark exam results. The summary is auto-generated from the checked rows — review it before proceeding.
guide.addSession.step5text: Write free text above and below the "The Next C/S:" line. The form is pre-filled with content from the previous session. Set the admin time field to record any additional administrative minutes.
guide.addSession.scanInboxText: Share PDFs from CamScanner (or any scanner app) directly to LP. Files whose name starts with "att" are treated as attachments. Session files are auto-loaded when you open the Add Session wizard.
guide.addSession.tip: Name your scanned files clearly. Files starting with "att" (e.g. "att pink sheet") become attachments. Everything else is treated as the session document.
guide.pcFolder.title: PC Folder
guide.pcFolder.sub: Browse and manage all documents in a PC’s folder
guide.pcFolder.step1text: Each PC folder has three sections: Front Cover, Back Cover, and WorkSheets. Front and Back Cover support a recursive subfolder tree. WorkSheets are organised by session date.
guide.pcFolder.step2text: Click any PDF to open it in the built-in viewer. Toggle dual-page mode from the toolbar to view two pages side by side. Use left/right arrows or keyboard shortcuts to navigate. The mode persists when switching files.
guide.pcFolder.step3text: Right-click files for options: Rename, Delete, Move, and Download. Right-click folders for: New Folder, Upload Files, and New Empty PDF. In WorkSheets, right-click a session to add Pink Sheets, Instructions, or Cramming pages.
guide.pcFolder.step4text: In Front Cover and Back Cover PDFs, right-click any page to open a 17-colour picker. Select a colour to set the page background — useful for visually categorising pages.
guide.pcFolder.step5text: Click the Summary button in the toolbar to open a rich-text editor (Quill) for the session’s folder summary. Write or update notes, then save.
guide.pcs.title: PCs (Clients)
guide.pcs.sub: Profiles, purchases, payments and session history
guide.pcs.step1text: Type a name or external ID in the Search box at the top of the left panel. The list filters instantly as you type.
guide.pcs.step2text: Each PC in the list shows a coloured badge — green means hours remaining, red means over-used. The list is sorted with lowest hours at the top so you can spot who needs attention.
guide.pcs.add1text: Press the green New PC button. Fill in the required fields — First Name and Last Name are mandatory. If a person with the same name already exists, an error appears before saving.
guide.pcs.add2text: Add phone, email, date of birth, gender, and origin. These can also be filled in later by clicking Edit on the PC’s detail card.
guide.pcs.table1text: The left-panel table has five columns: PC Name, Hours Left, Total Time, Visits, and Hrs Purchased. Click any column header to sort ascending or descending.
guide.pcs.tabs1text: Shows the latest 50 sessions with date, auditor, duration, admin time, free/billed status, and approval status. Draft sessions can be edited inline. Approved sessions are read-only.
guide.pcs.tabs2text: Displays active and finished courses for the selected PC. Active courses show the start date; click Done to mark a course as finished. Finished courses show both start and end dates.
guide.pcs.tabs3text: Edit personal details: first name, last name, phone, email, date of birth, gender, origin, and external ID. Changes are saved when you click Save Changes.
guide.pcs.pay1text: Select a PC, then click + Add Payment in the Payments card.
guide.pcs.pay2text: Auditing — enter hours bought and amount paid. These hours count toward the remaining hours balance. Course — select the course from the dropdown and enter the amount paid. The PC is automatically enrolled in the Academy course.
guide.pcs.pay3text: Registrar defaults to your own name — change it if someone else processed the payment. Referral is optional: set it if the PC was referred by someone.
guide.pcs.purchase1text: Select a PC, then click the Purchase button. A multi-item modal opens where you can add Auditing hours and/or Course enrolments in a single transaction.
guide.pcs.purchase2text: For Auditing items, enter hours bought and amount paid. For Course items, select the course from the dropdown and enter the amount. You can add multiple items of different types.
guide.pcs.purchase3text: Choose a payment method: Cash, Credit Card, Check, or Bank Transfer. This information appears in the admin Payments page for tracking.
guide.pcs.purchase4text: Sign on the signature pad (required) and optionally add notes. The signature is saved with the purchase and appears on the printed receipt.
guide.pcs.purchase5text: After saving, click Print to generate a clean A4 receipt showing all line items, totals, date, and the customer’s signature.
guide.pcs.pending1text: Below the detail tabs, any purchases awaiting admin approval appear as amber-coloured cards showing the item summary, date, and total amount.
guide.pcs.tip: Course payments automatically create an open enrolment in the Academy. You do not need to enrol the student manually.
guide.academy.title: Academy
guide.academy.sub: Daily attendance, courses and weekly reports
guide.academy.visit1text: Click the day pill at the top of the page (Mon, Tue, etc.). The pill turns green when that day has visits. You cannot add visits to future days.
guide.academy.visit2text: Type in the Search students box. Matching names appear as chips below. Click a chip to add a visit for that student on the selected day.
guide.academy.visit3text: In the visit list on the right, click the trash icon next to a student’s chip to remove their visit for the selected day.
guide.academy.new1text: Press the New Student button in the top-right of the navigation bar.
guide.academy.new2text: First name, Last name, and Course are all required. A person with the same full name cannot be added twice — the system will warn you. Select the Referral and Organization if known.
guide.academy.new3text: Saving the form automatically records a visit for the currently selected day, so you do not need to add it separately.
guide.academy.course1text: In the weekly student table, click the book icon on any student row. A modal opens showing their active and completed courses.
guide.academy.course2text: Click Finish next to an active course, pick the finish date, then confirm. The course moves to the completed list and the student’s active course column clears.
guide.academy.course3text: Use the Add Course dropdown at the bottom of the modal. Choose the course, set the start date, and click Add.
guide.academy.print1text: The Print button (top-right) generates a PDF for the currently displayed week. The PDF includes the student attendance table, referral and org breakdowns, each student’s active course with visit count, and a per-course summary table.
guide.academy.print2text: The PDF opens or downloads automatically. No printer is needed — you can save it or share the file directly.
guide.academy.chart1text: A bar chart at the bottom of the page shows weekly visit totals over the last 20 weeks. Hover over any bar to see the exact count.
guide.academy.ref1text: When adding a student, choose from four referral types: Don, Friend, Social, or Other. The referral breakdown appears in the printed weekly PDF.
guide.academy.noCourse1title: Adding a visit without a course
guide.academy.noCourse1text: If a student has no active course, a warning dialog appears asking whether to add the visit anyway. Click Add Anyway to proceed or Cancel to stop.
guide.academy.tip: The Active Course column shows the course name and the total number of academy visits since enrolment — e.g. Communication (12).
guide.statistics.title: Statistics
guide.statistics.step1text: Navigate to Statistics from the sidebar menu. The page displays visual charts summarising key metrics across the system.
guide.statistics.step2text: Charts are interactive — hover over data points for details. Use the week selector at the top to navigate between weeks.
guide.statistics.step3text: Below the charts, data tables provide detailed breakdowns you can review.
guide.statistics.step4text: A table ranks all staff members by total hours worked this week. Columns show Auditing hours, CS Solo hours, and Total.
guide.statistics.step5text: Breaks down auditing hours by organization (e.g. Haifa, Riga, Other), giving a quick view of where your clients come from.
guide.statistics.step6text: Four metric cards at the top show: Auditing Time (hours worked today), PCs (clients seen today), Students (academy visits today), and Attendance (unique visitors). The Week Totals row shows the same metrics summed for the entire week.
guide.statistics.step7text: A multi-series chart plots Auditing Time, Hours Remaining, Hours Purchased, and Sales over the last 20 weeks.
guide.management.title: Management
guide.management.auditor1text: Navigate to Management, then Auditors. The page shows all auditor profiles with their current status and grade.
guide.management.auditor2text: Click on an auditor to view or update their profile, including active/inactive status and auditor type (Regular, Solo, or both).
guide.management.auditor3text: When you select an auditor, four gradient cards appear: Total Sessions, Hours Worked, Free Sessions, and Last Session. These give a quick overview of the auditor’s activity.
guide.management.auditor4text: Fill in First Name, Last Name, and select a Grade from the dropdown above the auditor list, then click Add. The new auditor appears in the list immediately.
guide.management.auditor5text: In the auditor detail card, choose the Type: Inactive, Regular Only, Solo Only, or Regular + Solo. This controls whether the auditor appears in the dashboard tables and which sessions they can record.
guide.management.course1text: Navigate to Management, then Courses. Type the course name in the input row at the bottom of the Courses table and press Add or hit Enter.
guide.management.course2text: Click the pencil icon to rename a course inline, then save with Enter. Click the trash icon to delete a course.
guide.management.course3text: Below the Courses table, the Enrolments table shows every student enrolled in every course — including registrar, referral, amount paid, and academy visit count.
guide.management.member1text: Navigate to Management, then Members. A table displays all members with their name, last visited date, and status.
guide.management.member2text: Click column headers to sort by name, last visit date, or status. Use the search box to filter the list instantly.
guide.management.member3text: Click the red Deactivate button next to any active member to hide them across the entire system. Click the green Reactivate button to restore a deactivated member.
guide.management.member4text: Click the Add Member button at the top of the page. Fill in First Name (required), Last Name, and optionally an External ID. If a member with the same name exists, you must add an External ID to distinguish them.
guide.management.member5text: Each member row shows coloured badges indicating their roles: PC (client), Academy (student), and Staff. This helps identify at a glance who is active in which part of the system.
guide.admin.title: Admin
guide.admin.sub: Work review, purchases, payments, permissions and messaging
guide.admin.wr1text: Open Admin, then Work Review. Each session row shows the auditor, PC, date, duration, and status. Set the Rate and Salary fields, then click Approve.
guide.admin.wr2text: CS review rows appear alongside sessions. Set the CS Salary field and approve. Each session can have at most one CS review.
guide.admin.wr3text: Click a section header to collapse or expand it. This keeps the page manageable when reviewing many sessions at once.
guide.admin.purch1text: Open Admin, then Client Purchases. A filterable table shows every purchase with PC name, date, items, total amount, and approval status.
guide.admin.purch2text: Click Approve to confirm a pending purchase. Use Delete to soft-delete, or Restore to bring back a deleted purchase. Toggle Show Deleted to reveal removed entries.
guide.admin.purch3text: Filter by Pending/Approved status, set a date range, or toggle Include Approved to narrow results. Collapsible Auditing and Course tables group items by type.
guide.admin.purch4text: Rows for purchases whose payment has been deposited in the bank appear with a coloured tint, making it easy to distinguish deposited vs outstanding amounts.
guide.admin.pay1text: Open Admin, then Payments. Every payment is listed with its method (Cash, Credit Card, Check, Bank Transfer), amount, date, and associated PC.
guide.admin.pay2text: Payments that are overdue display a visual indicator so you can follow up quickly.
guide.admin.pay3text: Toggle the Money-in-bank switch to mark payments as deposited. Deposited payments can be shown or hidden using the filter at the top of the page. Sortable columns let you organise by any field.
guide.admin.perm1text: When a staff member’s PC list is updated, a permission request may appear here. Click Approve to grant access or Reject to deny it.
guide.admin.perm2text: Each auditor card has an Allow All toggle. When on, the auditor can work with any PC. When off, you can add individual PCs to their approved list using the dropdown at the bottom of their card.
guide.admin.perm3text: Each auditor’s permission card can be collapsed by clicking its header. This keeps the page tidy when managing many auditors.
guide.admin.msg1text: Open Admin, then Messaging. Compose a message and select which staff members should receive it.
guide.admin.msg2text: The system automatically sends a message to all Admin users whenever a new purchase is created, so no purchase goes unnoticed.
guide.soloUsers.title: Solo Users
guide.soloUsers.sub: Complete guide for solo auditors — scan, share, and submit sessions from your phone
guide.soloUsers.step1text: A solo user is a Type 3 auditor who audits themselves. You have your own dedicated login, your own PC folder, and a simplified workflow. When you log in, the system automatically recognises you as a solo user and tailors the interface accordingly — your PC is pre-selected and you go straight to adding sessions.
guide.soloUsers.step2title: One-time setup: Install LP on your phone
guide.soloUsers.step2text: Android: Open Chrome and navigate to the LP website. Tap the three-dot menu (⋮) in the top-right corner and select "Install app" (not "Add to Home Screen" — you must use "Install app"). This installs LP as a full app with its own icon and enables the Share feature so you can send scanned PDFs directly to LP. — iPhone/iPad: Open Safari and navigate to the LP website. Tap the Share button (the square with an upward arrow) at the bottom of the screen, scroll down and tap "Add to Home Screen", then tap "Add". Note: on iPhone, you cannot share files directly to LP from a scanner app. Instead, save the scanned PDF to your phone and upload it manually in the Add Session wizard using the file picker.
guide.soloUsers.step3text: Install a scanner app on your phone such as CamScanner, Adobe Scan, Microsoft Lens, or Genius Scan (all free). These apps use your phone's camera to scan paper documents and automatically convert them into clean, professional PDF files with shadows removed and pages straightened. Any scanner app that can share PDFs will work with LP.
guide.soloUsers.step4text: After your auditing session, open your scanner app and photograph each page of the session worksheet. Most scanner apps let you capture multiple pages into a single PDF. The app will automatically straighten the pages, remove shadows, and enhance text readability. Name the file using the format YYMMDD (for example, "250404" for April 4th, 2025). This is the recommended naming convention — LP will automatically recognise it as a date and use it for the session name.
guide.soloUsers.step5title: Sharing the session PDF to LP
guide.soloUsers.step5text: This is the key step. In your scanner app, find the finished PDF and tap the Share button (the icon with an arrow or three connected dots). Your phone will show a list of apps you can share to — look for "LP" or "LifePower" in the list and tap it. The PDF is instantly uploaded to your personal inbox on the LP server. You will see a confirmation screen showing: the file name, your username, and how long the file will be available (3 hours). That's it — the file is now on the server waiting for you.
guide.soloUsers.step6text: If you need to attach additional documents to your session (pink sheets, cramming sheets, instructions), the process is the same — scan and share. The only difference is the file name: name the file starting with the letters "att". For example: "att pink sheet", "att instructions", "att cramming", or even just "att1". Any file whose name starts with "att" (case-insensitive — "ATT", "Att", and "att" all work) will automatically be treated as an attachment, not a session file. Files without the "att" prefix are treated as the session file.
guide.soloUsers.step7text: Here is the full flow you'll follow after each session: (1) Open your scanner app and scan all pages of the session worksheet → save as PDF → tap Share → select LP. (2) If you have attachments, scan each one → name it starting with "att" → share to LP. (3) Open LP on your phone or computer → tap the Add Session button (the Go button in the header). Your session file and all attachments are already loaded automatically — you'll see them in the wizard without any manual uploading. (4) Fill in the Auditor Report Form (times, processes, tone arm reads). (5) Fill in the Next C/S sheet. (6) Tap Go. Done! Your session is saved to your folder and the inbox is cleared.
guide.soloUsers.step8text: If you shared files to LP but don't see them when you open the Add Session wizard (for example, if you navigated to a different page and came back), tap the "Pick from Inbox" button that appears below the file upload area. This manually re-loads any files waiting in your inbox on the server.
guide.soloUsers.step9text: Files in your inbox are automatically deleted after 3 hours if not used. When you press Go to submit a session, the entire inbox is cleared. Only one session file is kept at a time — if you share a new session PDF, it replaces the previous one. Attachments accumulate until the inbox is cleared or they expire.
guide.soloUsers.tip: Your login stays active for 21 days. As long as you open LP or share a file to LP at least once every three weeks, you will never need to log in again. If your login does expire, simply open LP in Chrome on your phone, log in, and then you can share files again immediately.
guide.settings.title: Settings
guide.settings.step1text: Open Settings from the header gear icon. Enter your current password, then type and confirm a new password (minimum 8 characters). Click Update Password to save.
guide.settings.step2text: In the Profile Picture section, click the upload area and choose an image (JPEG, PNG, GIF, or WebP, max 2 MB). The photo appears in the header. Click Remove Photo to delete it.
guide.tips.title: Tips & Tricks
guide.tips.enterText: In most inline forms (adding a course, editing a name) you can press Enter instead of clicking the Save button.
guide.tips.searchText: The PC list and Academy student list filter as you type — no need to press a search button.
guide.tips.redBadgeText: A red remaining-hours badge on a PC means they have used more hours than purchased. Follow up on a new payment.
guide.tips.visitCountText: The number in parentheses next to a course name — e.g. Communication (8) — counts all academy visits since the enrolment start date.
guide.tips.logoutText: Click the logout icon in the top-right header bar. Your session expires automatically after 12 hours of inactivity.
guide.tips.pdfText: Both the Dashboard and the Academy pages have a Print button that generates a polished PDF — great for weekly briefings.
guide.tips.collapseText: In Work Review and Client Purchases, click a section header to collapse or expand it — keeps long pages manageable.
guide.tips.moneyBankText: In Payments, toggle the Money-in-bank switch to show or hide deposited payments. Tinted rows indicate already-deposited amounts.
guide.tips.receiptText: Every purchase includes a signature pad. After saving, you can print a receipt with full line items and the customer’s signature.
guide.tips.dragDropText: In the Add Session wizard and PC Folder, you can drag files directly from your desktop onto the upload area instead of using the file picker.
guide.tips.camScannerText: Install LP as an app on your phone, then use CamScanner’s Share button to send scanned PDFs directly to your LP inbox. Files starting with "att" become attachments automatically.
guide.tips.arrowKeysTitle: Arrow keys in the Auditor Report
guide.tips.arrowKeysText: When filling in the Auditor Report Form, use arrow keys to move between cells quickly. This speeds up data entry for handling blocks.
guide.tips.settingsText: Change your password and upload a profile picture from the Settings page. Access it via the gear icon in the top header.
guide.tips.contextMenuText: On the Dashboard grid and in the PC Folder, right-click for context menus with quick actions like add session, rename, delete, or move files.
guide.tips.weekNavText: The Dashboard, Academy, and Statistics pages all share the same Thu-Wed week cycle. Use the Prev/Next arrows to browse past weeks.
guide.import.title: Import System
guide.import.sub: Bulk-import PC folders and set up sessions automatically
guide.import.bulk1text: From the Actions menu click Import. The wizard scans a folder tree on the server, detects PC folders by looking for WorkSheets, Front Cover, and Back Cover sub-folders, and builds a list of PCs to import.
guide.import.bulk2text: Any path containing the word "archive" is automatically skipped. Folders with "solo" in the name are flagged as solo PCs, and the import creates a Solo user account for them automatically.
guide.import.bulk3text: Click Go to start the import. The modal closes immediately and a floating toast tracks progress. When finished you'll see a green checkmark and the system navigates to the Home page.
guide.import.bulk4text: After import, check the Home dashboard for new PCs. Solo PCs automatically get a user account created with a default password. Imported sessions are marked so they can be filtered out of the admin work review.
guide.import.bulk5text: Folder names are cleaned automatically — words like solo, review, folder, and confidential are stripped when creating the PC name. Diacritics are normalised to ASCII characters.
guide.import.names1title: Scan folder names without importing content
guide.import.names1text: Tick the Names Only checkbox before scanning. The import will find PC folder names at any depth, deduplicate them, and show a preview table. Archives are sorted to the bottom.
guide.import.names2text: In Names Only mode, each row has its own Go button so you can import PCs one at a time. The result (Created / Already exists / Error) appears inline next to each row.
guide.import.names3text: After creating PCs via Names Only, verify the entries on the PCs page. Solo-detected PCs will have a Solo user account already set up and ready to use.
guide.import.tip: Tip: Names Only mode is great for quickly creating PC entries from an existing folder structure without importing session files.
guide.sessionManager.title: Session Manager
guide.sessionManager.sub: Inspect, edit, and manage all sessions from one place
guide.sessionManager.pc1text: From the Actions dropdown choose Session Manager. Select a PC from the dropdown to see all its sessions. The table shows session ID, date, name, auditor, duration, free status, and approval status.
guide.sessionManager.pc2text: Click any session row to open the detail editor. The left card shows session fields (name, date, duration, admin time, charge, rate, salary, free checkbox, status). The right card shows the CS review if one exists (CS name, review length, status, notes, salary, charged rate).
guide.sessionManager.pc3text: In the detail view click Delete Session. A two-step confirmation appears: first click Yes I want to delete, then type the word "delete" to confirm. This permanently removes the session, all CS reviews, folder summaries, attachments, and worksheet files.
guide.sessionManager.solo1text: Click the Solo button to switch to solo view. This shows all solo sessions (where no auditor is assigned) across all PCs. Use the time filter pills (3d, 7d, 30d, ALL) to control how far back to look. Default is 7 days.
guide.sessionManager.solo2text: The CS Free/Bill column shows the current flag from the CS review. Rows highlighted in amber have a CS review but no Free or Bill flag set — these are likely mistakes where the CS forgot to mark the session. You can change the flag inline using the dropdown.
guide.sessionManager.solo3text: After changing a Free/Bill flag, a green save button appears next to the dropdown. Click it to save immediately without opening the full detail editor. Click a row to open the full detail view for deeper editing.
guide.sessionManager.add1text: Click the green Add button (available in both PC and Solo views). Fill in the PC, Auditor (choose Solo for no auditor), CS (choose None to skip), date, session length in minutes, admin time, and optional notes.
guide.sessionManager.add2text: When you save, the system generates a unique session name (e.g. 260413 Added manual session 1), creates a placeholder PDF with 'Dummy session' text, and saves it to the PC's WorkSheets folder. A CS review row is also created automatically.
guide.sessionManager.tip: Tip: The Solo view is especially useful for administrators to quickly find and fix sessions where the CS forgot to click Free or Bill.
guide.dashboard.step9text: If you have the Senior CS role, a Team CS Review section appears showing all CS staff with their pending session counts. Expand each staff member to see their individual queue.
guide.dashboard.step10text: Below the weekly overview and remarks, a bar chart shows your total hours per week going back several months. This helps you spot trends in your workload over time.
guide.dashboard.step11text: On mobile devices, solo users see two animated buttons at the top: Add Session (blue, opens the session wizard) and Open Folder (purple, navigates directly to your PC folder). These provide quick access without navigating menus.
guide.dashboard.step12text: Click any cell in the weekly grid to open a detail panel below the table. From here you can view session details, edit session info, add new sessions or CS reviews, and manage general CS work — all without leaving the dashboard.
guide.pcFolder.view1text: Click the dual-page toggle in the toolbar to view two pages side-by-side. Use the left/right arrows or keyboard to navigate. This mode is great for comparing documents or reading spread layouts.
guide.pcFolder.view2text: Use the page controls in the toolbar to jump between pages. Zoom in/out to read fine print. The viewer remembers your zoom level and dual-page preference when switching between files.
guide.pcFolder.annot1text: Right-click any page in Front Cover or Back Cover to open the color picker with 17 preset colors. Choose a color and click Save to apply it. The color is preserved as a vector overlay, keeping file sizes small.
guide.pcFolder.annot2text: Use the toolbar buttons to switch between Select, Draw (freehand), and Text modes. Draw annotations appear as a transparent overlay on top of the original PDF content. Click Save to persist your annotations.
guide.pcFolder.sheet1text: Right-click a session in the worksheet list to add a Pink Sheet (pink), Instruct (white), or Cramming (green) attachment. These are special PDF pages that appear as sub-items under the session.
guide.pcFolder.sheet2text: Right-click any attachment (pink sheet, instruct, or cramming) to rename or delete it. The rename dialog lets you change the suffix while keeping the session prefix intact.
guide.pcFolder.fileOp1text: Upload PDF, DOC, or DOCX files. Word documents are automatically converted to PDF in the background with a progress toast notification showing conversion status (spinner, then green checkmark or red error).
guide.pcFolder.fileOp2text: Right-click a page in a program file (Front/Back Cover) and choose Insert Form to pick from available templates. The template pages are inserted at the selected position.
guide.pcFolder.fileOp3text: Right-click a page and choose Insert file above to upload a PDF or DOC file. Its pages are inserted before the selected page, scaled to match the document width.
guide.pcFolder.fileOp4text: XLSX files open in a built-in spreadsheet editor. You can view, edit cell values, add rows, and delete rows. Changes are saved back to the Excel file on the server.
guide.pcFolder.fileOp5text: Click the Summary button in the toolbar to open a rich text editor for the session's folder summary. Write notes about the session that are saved separately from the PDF files.
guide.admin.diag1text: The User Activity card shows all users with their online status, last action, last active time, and activity recency. Click column headers to sort. Click History to view a detailed audit log for any user.
guide.admin.diag2text: Browse and inspect all database tables, run diagnostic checks, and view system configuration. Each table shows row counts and can be expanded to view contents.
guide.admin.backup1text: The Backup page (Admin > Backup) lets you download a complete database backup and PC folder archives. Authenticate with your password, then choose System Backup (database) or User/Server Backup (folder files).
guide.admin.backup2text: Backups show a progress bar with phase indicators. You can cancel a backup in progress. If the connection drops, the backup continues on the server and you can reconnect.
guide.admin.salary1text: The Salary page lets you configure auditor hourly rates, CS hourly rates, and session charge rates per PC. Rates are shown in the PC's currency (ILS, USD, EUR) and used for billing calculations.
guide.settings.step3text: In Settings, click Take Photo to open the camera. Position yourself and click the snap button. The photo is saved as your avatar and appears in the header, messages, and session lists. You can also upload a photo file instead.
guide.settings.step4text: Toggle between Light and Dark mode using the sun/moon icon in the header. Switch between English and Hebrew using the flag icons. Your preference is saved and persists across sessions.
guide.settings.step5text: Visit the Shortcuts page to create custom Ctrl+key shortcuts. You can assign up to 26 shortcuts (A-Z) that trigger quick actions throughout the application.
guide.tips.sessionMgrText: Use the Solo view in Session Manager to quickly find sessions where the CS forgot to mark Free or Bill. Amber-highlighted rows need attention.
guide.tips.transferText: Use Actions > Transfer Balance to move unused auditing hours from one PC to another. The system checks balances and prevents over-transfers.
guide.tips.importText: Use Import > Names Only mode to quickly create PC entries from an existing folder tree without importing any files.
guide.tips.dualPageText: Toggle dual-page mode in the PDF viewer to see two pages side-by-side. Navigate with arrow keys for fast document review.
guide.tips.bgColorText: Right-click a page in Front/Back Cover to apply a background color. The color is saved efficiently as a vector overlay, keeping your file sizes small.
guide.addSession.arf1text: Times must be exactly 4 digits in HH:MM format (e.g. 09:30, not 9:30). The system auto-formats as you type — entering 0930 becomes 09:30. Invalid times are highlighted in red and block the Next button.
guide.addSession.arf2text: Each ARF row has four columns: Process (what was done), Time (HH:MM), Tone Arm Reads (numeric with decimal), and Results & Comments (free text). Press Enter in the Results column to insert a new row below.
guide.addSession.scan1title: Share a PDF from your phone
guide.addSession.scan1text: If LP is installed as an app (PWA) on your Android device, you can share scanned PDFs directly to LP from any scanning app. The file appears in your Scan Inbox.
guide.addSession.scan2text: In the Add Session wizard (step 2), click Pick from Inbox to load files from your scan inbox. Session files and attachments are detected automatically based on naming.
guide.addSession.docConv1text: When you upload a DOC, DOCX, ODT, or RTF file, it is automatically converted to PDF in the background. A toast notification shows the conversion progress — spinner while converting, green checkmark on success.
guide.addSession.multiArf1text: Click Add Session ARF to create a new independent ARF table within the same session. Each table has its own rows, time range, and duration calculation. The total session length is the sum of all table durations.
guide.admin.sessMgr1text: Open Session Manager from the Actions dropdown. This gives you a powerful view of all sessions with the ability to edit, delete, and create sessions for any PC.
guide.admin.sessMgr2text: Switch to Solo mode to see all solo sessions across PCs. Quickly identify missing Free/Bill flags highlighted in amber, and fix them with the inline dropdown.
guide.admin.backup3text: Server Backup downloads all PC folder files as a compressed archive. This may take several minutes for large installations. Progress is tracked in real time.
guide.dashboard.teamCs1text: If you have the Senior CS role, a Team CS Review panel appears showing all CS staff members and their pending review counts.
guide.dashboard.teamCs2text: Click a staff member to expand their pending sessions. You can toggle show/hide completed reviews and filter by time range.
guide.dashboard.weeklyHistory1text: A bar chart below the weekly overview shows your total hours per week going back several months. This helps you spot workload trends and plan ahead.
guide.dashboard.mobile1text: On mobile, a blue animated Add Session button appears at the top of the page. Tap it to open the session wizard without navigating to the Actions menu.
guide.dashboard.mobile2text: Solo users see a purple Open Folder button below Add Session on mobile. It navigates directly to your PC folder for quick file access.
guide.dashboard.detailPanel1text: Click any cell in the weekly grid to open a detail panel below. View session details, edit session info, add new sessions or CS reviews, and manage general CS work — all without leaving the dashboard.
guide.pcFolder.docView1text: Click the dual-page toggle in the toolbar to view two pages side-by-side. Use left/right arrows or keyboard to navigate. Great for comparing documents.
guide.pcFolder.docView2text: Use the page controls to jump between pages. Zoom in/out for fine print. The viewer remembers your preferences when switching files.
guide.pcFolder.docView3text: The left panel shows a tree view of all files organised by section. Use the session navigator to filter and search within WorkSheets.
guide.pcFolder.wsMgmt1title: Add Pink Sheets, Instruct, and Cramming
guide.pcFolder.wsMgmt1text: Right-click a session in the worksheet list and choose Add Pink Sheet, Add Instruct, or Add Cramming. These create special colored attachment pages under the session.
guide.pcFolder.wsMgmt2text: Right-click any attachment (pink sheet, instruct, or cramming) and choose Rename to change its name while keeping the session prefix.
guide.pcFolder.wsMgmt3text: Right-click a pink sheet, instruct, or cramming attachment and choose Delete to remove it. A confirmation dialog appears before deletion.
guide.pcFolder.fileOps1text: Upload PDF, DOC, or DOCX files. Word documents are automatically converted to PDF with a toast notification showing progress.
guide.pcFolder.fileOps2text: Right-click a page in Front/Back Cover and choose Insert Form to pick from available templates. Pages are inserted at the selected position.
guide.pcFolder.fileOps3text: Right-click a page and choose Insert file above to upload and insert a PDF or DOC before the selected page, scaled to match the document.
guide.pcFolder.fileOps4text: XLSX files open in a built-in spreadsheet editor where you can view, edit, add rows, and delete rows. Changes save back to the server.
guide.pcs.find1text: Click PCs in the sidebar to see the full list of client profiles. Use the search bar to filter by name.
guide.pcs.find2text: Click a PC row to open the detail view with tabs for sessions, purchases, payments, courses, and more.
guide.sessionManager.pcView1text: Choose a PC from the dropdown to see all its sessions. The table shows ID, date, name, auditor, duration, free status, and approval status with pagination for large lists.
guide.sessionManager.pcView2text: If a PC has more than 50 sessions, use the Prev/Next buttons at the bottom to page through them.
guide.sessionManager.pcView3text: Click any row to open the full detail editor with session fields on the left and CS review fields on the right.
guide.sessionManager.soloView1text: Click the Solo button to see all solo sessions across all PCs. Use the time pills (3d, 7d, 30d, ALL) to control the lookback period.
guide.sessionManager.soloView2text: Rows highlighted in amber have a CS review but no Free or Bill flag — these need attention. Change the flag inline with the dropdown and click the green save button.
guide.sessionManager.addSess1text: Click the green Add button. Fill in PC, Auditor (Solo for no auditor), CS (None or a staff member), date, session length in minutes, admin time, free checkbox, and notes.
guide.sessionManager.addSess2text: The system creates a unique session name, generates a placeholder PDF, and saves it to the PC's WorkSheets folder. A CS review row is also created automatically.
guide.sessionManager.detail1text: The left card shows all session fields: name, date, duration, admin seconds, charge, rate, salary, status, free checkbox, and notes. Change any value and click Save.
guide.sessionManager.detail2text: The right card shows the CS review: reviewer name, review length, status, notes, salary, and charged rate. Reassign the CS reviewer using the dropdown.
guide.sessionManager.detail3text: Click Delete Session for a two-step confirmation. First click 'Yes, I want to delete', then type 'delete' to confirm. This permanently removes the session and all related data.
guide.settings.camera1text: In Settings, click Take Photo to open your device camera. Position yourself and click the snap button to capture. The photo becomes your avatar shown in the header and messages.
guide.settings.camera2text: You can also upload a photo file (JPG, PNG, WebP) instead of using the camera. Click the delete button to remove your avatar and return to the default initial.
guide.settings.theme1text: Toggle between Light and Dark mode using the sun/moon icon in the header bar. Your preference is saved and persists across sessions.
guide.settings.lang1text: Click the flag icons in the header to switch between English and Hebrew. The entire interface updates immediately.
guide.settings.shortcuts1text: Visit the Shortcuts page to create up to 26 custom shortcuts (Ctrl+A through Ctrl+Z) for quick actions throughout the application.
guide.tips.soloSessionMgrText: Use the Solo view in Session Manager to find sessions where CS forgot to mark Free or Bill. Amber rows need attention.
guide.tips.rightClickText: Most items have context menus — files, folders, pages, sessions, column headers. Right-click to discover actions.
guide.tips.transferBalanceText: Use Actions > Transfer Balance to move unused auditing hours between PCs. The system validates balances.
guide.tips.importFoldersText: Use Import with Names Only mode to create PC entries from an existing folder tree without importing files.
academy.noActiveCourseMsg: is not currently enrolled in any course. Do you still want to add a visit?
pcs.signatureReminder: Don't forget to sign before submitting
purchases.title: Purchase Approval
purchases.subtitle: Review and approve pending purchases
pcs.delete.title: Delete Purchase
pcs.delete.newDisabled: Save the purchase first before deleting
pcs.delete.warning: This will permanently and irreversibly delete the purchase and every related row — payments, payment methods, purchase items, and active course enrollments.
pcs.delete.typeHint: Type this word to confirm permanent deletion:
pcs.delete.deleting: Deleting purchase and all related rows…
transfer.title: Transfer Balance
purchases.amountMismatch: Payment total does not match items total
pcs.confirmDeleteMsg: This purchase will be marked as deleted. An admin can restore it later.
pcs.confirmFinishCourseMsg: Are you sure you want to mark "{0}" as finished?
pcs.confirmReopenCourseMsg: Are you sure you want to reopen "{0}"?
pcs.noSignatureNote: Purchase will be saved without a signature
actions.title: Actions
actions.noFoldersMsg: You haven't been assigned to any PC folders yet.
actions.contactAdmin: Contact your administrator to request access.
actions.memoSaveNote: The report will be saved as a single A4 PDF page in the PC's WorkSheets folder.
pcs.courseCompletionsSub: Courses finished within the selected period, grouped by PC
pcs.selectDateRange: Select a date range and press Apply.
pcs.noCompletionsInPeriod: No course completions found in this period.
pcs.columnGuide: Column Guide — how each value is calculated
guide.actionsMenu.title: Actions Menu
guide.actionsMenu.sub: Every shortcut in the Actions dropdown (header)
guide.actionsMenu.lead: The Actions dropdown in the top header collects every quick-action in one place — adding sessions, writing to folders, scheduling meetings, transferring balances and more. The list of options depends on your role (Solo users see a trimmed set).
guide.actionsMenu.addSessionText: Opens the full Add Session wizard. Solo users also get a large animated Go button at the top of the dashboard on mobile.
guide.actionsMenu.addNextCsText: Creates a Next C/S sheet without a full session. Pick the PC and date, then fill the free-text editor. Step 2 of the wizard now includes an Auditor dropdown — the selected auditor is written to the session's AuditorId (so the memo-session counts toward that auditor's weekly totals). The dropdown lists all active Auditors, CS and SeniorCS (Solo users are excluded). For solo logged-in users the dropdown is hidden and the signature stays on their own name.
guide.actionsMenu.openFolderText: Opens the PC folder for a selected PC in a new tab. Useful for jumping straight into a client's documents without leaving the current page.
guide.actionsMenu.writeSummaryText: Opens a Quill rich-text editor to append notes to a session's folder summary. The summary is stored per session and shows up in the PC Folder toolbar's Summary button.
guide.actionsMenu.addMemoText: Writes a free-text report and saves it as a single A4 PDF page inside the PC's WorkSheets folder. Used for non-session notes (phone calls, admin activities, etc.) you want permanently filed in the client's folder.
guide.actionsMenu.uploadFrontCoverText: Quick upload path to drop a PDF/DOC/DOCX into the PC's Front Cover section without navigating the tree. Word documents are auto-converted to PDF with a background toast.
guide.actionsMenu.scheduleMeetingText: Opens the meeting scheduler. Choose date, time, PC and auditor, and mark it as Auditing or Academy. The meeting shows up on the Schedule page calendar color-coded by auditor.
guide.actionsMenu.finishGradeText: Marks a client's current auditing grade as finished. Used when the PC completes a course of auditing and is ready to move to the next grade. The finish date is recorded and shows up in the PC's session/course history.
guide.actionsMenu.transferBalanceText: Moves unused auditing hours or money between two PCs (or between wallets). Pick source wallet, target wallet and the amount. The system enforces currency matching and prevents over-transfers.
guide.actionsMenu.sessionMgrText: Opens the admin Session Manager for deep edits on any session. See the Session Manager chapter for full details.
guide.actionsMenu.printReportsText: Generates a PDF with weekly staff-level reports — grid totals, CS queues, extra effort — for the currently selected week. Useful for weekly briefings.
guide.actionsMenu.effortLogText: Opens the Effort Log entry modal directly, so you can log non-session work time without navigating to the Effort Log page. See the Effort Log chapter for the full flow.
guide.actionsMenu.assignPcText: Adds a PC to your dashboard for the current week. Pick a role (Auditor, CS Solo, CS) then choose the PC. PCs with pending reviews are highlighted in green in the picker.
guide.actionsMenu.importText: Starts the bulk folder import wizard — see the Import chapter. Admin-only.
guide.actionsMenu.tip: Tip: the Actions dropdown is the fastest way to do almost anything without navigating. Solo users get a reduced menu focused on Add Session, Open Folder and Write Summary.
guide.effortLog.title: Effort Log
guide.effortLog.sub: Log non-session work time (admin, training, meetings)
guide.effortLog.lead: The Effort Log page (and the Add Effort Log action) lets staff record time spent on things other than billable sessions — like admin tasks, internal meetings, training courses, or administrative follow-up. These entries feed the Extra Effort table in the weekly dashboard and the staff leaderboards in Statistics.
guide.effortLog.step1text: Navigate to Effort Log from the sidebar, or use Actions → Add Effort Log for a one-off entry without leaving the current page.
guide.effortLog.step2text: Click New Entry. Fill the effort date, pick a Category (e.g. Admin, Training, Meeting), enter the length in minutes and optionally a short Notes field. Save. The entry is attached to the logged-in user; admins can also log on behalf of other staff.
guide.effortLog.step3text: Categories come from the lookup table lkp_effort_categories. Common examples are Admin, Staff Meeting, Training, Internal Session, External Meeting, Sick Leave. An admin can add or rename categories.
guide.effortLog.step4text: Each row in the log has an edit pencil and a delete button. Editing re-opens the modal with the existing values. Deleting is immediate — there is no soft-delete on effort entries.
guide.effortLog.step5title: How it shows up on the dashboard
guide.effortLog.step5text: The Home dashboard's Extra Effort table aggregates effort entries for the current week (bucketed by the entry's creation date). Each person who logged effort this week gets a column; each day gets a row. Totals feed the weekly grand total.
guide.effortLog.tip: Tip: use the Effort Log for things that take real time but aren't a session — otherwise your weekly hours will look artificially low. Admin buckets the server at midnight so midnight entries may land on the previous day if the server timezone isn't configured to Israel.
guide.schedule.title: Schedule
guide.schedule.sub: Plan Auditing and Academy meetings on a calendar
guide.schedule.lead: The Schedule page shows a week view of all planned meetings — both 1-on-1 auditing meetings and Academy course meetings. Each auditor's meetings are color-coded so the grid is readable at a glance. You can also reach the scheduler modal directly from Actions → Schedule Meeting.
guide.schedule.step1text: The week starts on Thursday, like the rest of the app. Use the Prev/Next arrows to move between weeks. Each day is a column; each hour is a row. Meetings appear as colored blocks.
guide.schedule.step2text: Click an empty slot, or open Actions → Schedule Meeting. Pick the date, start time, duration, PC (for Auditing meetings) or course (for Academy meetings) and the auditor. Save. The block appears immediately on the calendar.
guide.schedule.step3text: Above the calendar, each active auditor is shown as a colored pill. The same color is used on their meeting blocks. Hover over a block to see the full title, PC name, auditor and time.
guide.schedule.step4text: Right-click any meeting block to open its context menu with Edit and Delete options. Edit re-opens the scheduler modal; Delete removes the meeting after a confirmation.
guide.schedule.step5text: Auditing meetings are tied to a specific PC and auditor. Academy meetings are tied to a course and can have multiple students — they appear in the course chart on the Academy page. Use the meeting-type toggle in the modal to switch between them.
guide.messaging.title: Messaging
guide.messaging.sub: Internal messages to staff and admin
guide.messaging.lead: LPM has a built-in internal messaging system. Messages are one-to-one or broadcast to multiple staff, appear as a red badge on the envelope icon in the header, and persist until acknowledged.
guide.messaging.step1text: Click the envelope icon in the top header. The inbox modal opens with your pending messages, each showing sender, timestamp and body. A red number badge on the envelope icon shows how many are unread.
guide.messaging.step2text: Click OK next to any single message to mark it as read. If you have many pending messages, click OK All to mark the entire inbox as acknowledged in one go. Acknowledged messages disappear from the badge count.
guide.messaging.step3text: In the inbox modal click New Message. Select one or more recipients (any active staff member), type a short message and click Send. The message appears on the recipient's envelope badge within seconds.
guide.messaging.step4text: The system sends automatic messages to all Admins on key events: a new purchase is created, a session is edited after approval, or a staff permission request is pending. This ensures important activity doesn't go unnoticed.
guide.wallets.title: Wallets & Transfers
guide.wallets.sub: Manage money balances per PC and transfer between them
guide.wallets.lead: Every PC has one or more wallets — effectively separate balance buckets with their own currency (ILS, USD, EUR). Each session is billed against a specific wallet, and you can transfer unused hours or money between wallets or between PCs.
guide.wallets.step1text: A wallet is a per-PC balance container. A PC might have a default ILS wallet for local sessions and a USD wallet for a foreign relative paying for them separately. Wallets track hours purchased, hours used, and remaining balance in the wallet's currency.
guide.wallets.step2text: Open a PC, go to the Balance tab (or the Wallet section on the PC card), and click + New Wallet. Pick a currency, add optional notes (e.g. "Paid by Mother"), save. Existing wallets can be renamed or have their notes edited the same way; their balance cannot be edited directly — only via purchases, sessions and transfers.
guide.wallets.step3text: When you add a session for a PC, it's billed to the PC's default wallet unless you pick a different one. The default is the first wallet created; you can set any wallet as default from its row menu.
guide.wallets.step4text: Use Actions → Transfer Balance. Pick a source wallet and a target wallet (can be the same PC or a different PC), enter the amount to move. The source and target currencies must match — the system blocks cross-currency transfers. A transfer creates matching deduct/credit entries so the audit trail is preserved.
guide.wallets.step5text: The PC's Balance / Payments tabs show each wallet with hours purchased, hours used, remaining balance and rate. Admin's Payments and Purchases pages also filter by wallet so you can see the cash flow for a specific currency bucket.
guide.wallets.tip: Tip: if a family member pays for sessions for multiple relatives, open a wallet per payer and charge the sessions to the matching wallet. This keeps each payer's balance clear without needing separate PCs.
guide.tips.auditorDropdownTitle: Pick the auditor on Next C/S
guide.tips.auditorDropdownText: The standalone Add Next C/S wizard now has an Auditor dropdown on step 2. Select any active Auditor, CS or SeniorCS — the memo-session is attributed to them so it counts toward their weekly totals.
guide.tips.auditingStatus7Text: The "My Auditing Status" table on the dashboard now defaults to the last 7 days (previously 21). Change the range from the filter pills at the top of the table.
guide.tips.createdAtBucketingText: The dashboard's weekly overview tables bucket work by the session's creation time (not its session date). Sessions created today land in today's column regardless of the session date field.
guide.tips.sidebarResizeText: In PC Folder side-by-side view, opening or closing the file-tree sidebar now rescales the pages automatically. Also triggers on window resize and pane layout changes.
guide.tips.manualSessionIndexText: In Session Manager, adding a manual session auto-picks the first free index ("YYMMDD Added manual session 1", 2, 3 …) and only writes the DB row if the PDF file was actually saved. No more orphaned sessions on silent save failures.
guide.tips.cinematicReadingText: In PC Folder, the first-time expand of a session to dual-page + maximize plays a short "Cinematic Reading" animation — a visual cue that you've entered immersive reading mode. The mode itself is otherwise identical to regular dual-page.
guide.tips.changePasswordText: Settings → Change Password asks for your current password, a new one (8+ chars) and a confirmation. The form refuses to submit if the two new values don't match. Changing password does NOT log you out of other devices.
guide.tips.verifyDeviceText: When logging in from a new device (or after cookie expiry), the system may ask for an email OTP code. The Verify Device page holds a single input for the code; paste or type the 6 digits you received and click Verify.
guide.tips.scanConfirmText: After you share a PDF to LP from a scanner app, the Scan Received page shows the filename, your username and the 3-hour expiry window. You can close that tab — the file is already saved to the server and ready for the Add Session wizard.
guide.tips.effortBucketCreatedText: The Extra Effort table in the weekly dashboard buckets entries by the CreatedAt of each effort log entry (same rule as the sessions grid). If you log effort today, it appears in today's column even if its effort-date field is set to a different day.
